markers_included: ~640000 SNPs incl MTHFR (C677T A1298C) COMT MAO-A FOLR1 region MTRR MTR BHMT VDR CBS GST APOE
what_it_measures: Genome-wide SNP genotyping (raw data downloadable as txt)
what_results_indicate: Provides raw SNP data for downstream methylation/detox/folate analysis via third-party tools
specific_use_cases: Foundation genetic screen; raw data downloadable for free reanalysis; lowest-friction entry point
contraindications: Bankruptcy 2025-26 may affect long-term data availability
notes: Bankruptcy raises data-storage uncertainty; raw data download still active 2026. Consider downloading raw .txt within first 30 days post-results.

markers_included: ~700000 SNPs incl MTHFR COMT VDR FOLR1 region
what_results_indicate: Equivalent foundation for third-party SNP analysis
specific_use_cases: Alternative to 23andMe if bankruptcy concerns; sometimes cheaper sales
notes: Coverage of methylation SNPs is comparable to 23andMe but variant set differs slightly.

markers_included: MTHFR C677T A1298C COMT V158M MAO-A R297R MTRR A66G MTR A2756G CBS C699T VDR Taq/Fok GST/GPX
what_it_measures: Post-processing of raw SNP data for methylation + Phase I/II detox pathways
what_results_indicate: Identifies methylation-cycle SNPs guiding leucovorin/methyl-B12/methionine strategy
specific_use_cases: Free methylation report; $10-15 detox add-on; widely used in FM community
contraindications: Educational only; results require clinical interpretation
notes: Output overlays SNP genotypes onto Yasko-style methylation diagram. Common starting point.

markers_included: Methylation + detoxification + sleep/anxiety SNPs; supplement avoidance list
what_it_measures: Nutrigenomic post-processing of raw SNP data
what_results_indicate: Methylation tier and supplement-to-avoid list
specific_use_cases: Detoxification + Methylation report ~$57; carrier-status ~$22
contraindications: Educational; clinician interpretation recommended
notes: Stronger on supplement compatibility lists than Genetic Genie. Useful complement.

sample_collection: upload raw or order Sequencing.com kit
markers_included: Methylation + folate cycle + neurotransmitter + detox pathway map
what_it_measures: Pathway-mapped methylation interpretation with intervention pairing
what_results_indicate: Provides pathway-context view of SNPs (more clinical than Genetic Genie raw output)
specific_use_cases: Dr Ben Lynch-developed; widely used in FM autism practice
contraindications: Vendor-specific interpretation may be over-confident
notes: Pathway-context output makes it easier for parents to act on than raw SNP lists.

markers_included: 500+ SNPs across methylation neurotransmitter mitochondrial detox GABA glutamate cell signaling
what_it_measures: Clinical-grade nutrigenomic panel with practitioner-only access
what_results_indicate: Maps to specific supplementation + medication-avoidance strategies
specific_use_cases: Used in MAPS / functional autism practices; richer than DTC SNP tools
contraindications: Practitioner-only; not insurance-covered
notes: Most comprehensive nutrigenomic panel marketed specifically for autism/neurodev.

markers_included: 1.9M CNVs + 750K SNPs; detects deletions/duplications incl 22q11.2 16p11.2 15q11-q13 etc
what_it_measures: Genome-wide CNV detection at high resolution
what_results_indicate: Identifies known autism-associated CNVs (15-20% diagnostic yield)
specific_use_cases: ACMG first-tier test for unexplained DD/ID/ASD; standard of care
notes: Insurance typically covers with DD/ID/ASD diagnosis. Order before WES.

test_name: Fragile X (FMR1 CGG repeat) PCR + Southern blot
markers_included: FMR1 CGG repeat count + methylation status
what_it_measures: Detects premutation (55-200) and full mutation (>200) FMR1 expansions
what_results_indicate: Diagnoses Fragile X syndrome (1-2% of autism cases)
specific_use_cases: ACMG first-tier; recommended for unexplained ID/DD; especially boys with intellectual deficit + macrocephaly
notes: Usually covered by insurance with autism/ID diagnosis. Standard of care.

markers_included: ~20000 protein-coding genes; detects pathogenic variants across SFARI Tier 1+2
what_results_indicate: Identifies single-gene autism causes; diagnostic yield ~25-30% if CMA negative
specific_use_cases: Second-tier after CMA+FXS; AAP recommends as first-line for DD/ID
contraindications: Long turnaround; variant-of-unknown-significance burden
notes: Trio (proband+parents) is more interpretable but more expensive. GeneDx offers ExomeDx.

markers_included: 76 markers: yeast (arabinose tartaric) Clostridia (HPHPA 4-cresol DHPPA) oxalates Krebs cycle B-vitamin neurotransmitter (HVA VMA 5-HIAA quinolinic) fatty acid oxidation
what_results_indicate: Yeast/Clostridia overgrowth + Krebs cycle dysfunction + neurotransmitter metabolites
specific_use_cases: Tier-1 foundational FM test; HPHPA elevation guides Clostridia treatment
notes: Order-without-physician available. Excellent screening for mito + microbiome.

markers_included: 65 organic acids: yeast bacteria oxalates Krebs cycle B-vitamin neurotransmitter markers
what_results_indicate: Equivalent diagnostic profile to Mosaic OAT
specific_use_cases: Alternative to Mosaic OAT; Genova bills insurance ($179 copay option)
notes: Out-of-pocket $319; insurance option submitted via Genova-Connect.

markers_included: SAM SAH SAM/SAH ratio methionine homocysteine cystathionine cysteine
what_results_indicate: Identifies undermethylator vs overmethylator phenotype + functional B12/folate status
specific_use_cases: Walsh-protocol-aligned; the most informative single methylation test
contraindications: Requires clinician order; venous draw can be hard in young children
notes: SAM/SAH ratio is the single most informative methylation biomarker per Walsh framework.

markers_included: Homocysteine MMA plasma B12 RBC folate active B12 (holotranscobalamin)
what_results_indicate: Functional B12 + folate status; homocysteine reflects net cycle activity
specific_use_cases: Insurance-covered baseline; covers most of what DD methylation panel does at fraction of cost
notes: Order: homocysteine MMA plasma B12 RBC folate. P5P optional. The 90% solution.

provider: Religious Sisters of Mercy / Iliad Neurosciences
what_it_measures: Anti-folate-receptor antibodies blocking cerebral folate transport
what_results_indicate: Positive FRAA predicts leucovorin responsiveness (Frye 2018 RCT)
specific_use_cases: Single most important biomarker for INT-0001 leucovorin decision
contraindications: Specialty lab; longer turnaround
notes: FRAA-positive ~70-75% of autistic children per multiple studies. Strong leucovorin predictor.

what_results_indicate: Elevated lactate or L:P >25 suggests mitochondrial dysfunction
specific_use_cases: Tier-1 mitochondrial screen; foundational for PHE-0002 stratification
contraindications: Lactate samples must be handled correctly (deproteinized rapidly); tourniquet artifact common
notes: Strict pre-analytical handling required. Send to hospital lab not local LabCorp draw if possible.

markers_included: 40+ acylcarnitine species C0 (free carnitine) through C18
what_results_indicate: Distinctive AC profiles in ~17% of autistic children per Frye 2013
specific_use_cases: Newborn-screen tier panel covers ~40 species; specific patterns indicate FAO defects
notes: Mayo is gold standard; LabCorp/Quest also offer.

what_it_measures: Carnitine substrate availability for fatty acid transport into mito
what_results_indicate: Low free carnitine indicates supplementation may help
specific_use_cases: Pairs with acylcarnitine profile
notes: Order alongside lactate/pyruvate + acylcarnitine for complete mito screen.

markers_included: 40+ amino acids including methionine taurine glycine glutamate GABA tryptophan
what_results_indicate: Catches inborn errors of metabolism + functional B6/B12/methylation deficits
specific_use_cases: Quest test code 767; identifies inborn errors of metabolism + Hannah Poling slot signals
contraindications: Fasting required (may be hard in young kids)
notes: ~$200 OOP at Quest. Reveals MSUD PKU homocystinuria + functional B6 deficits.

what_results_indicate: Low CoQ10 indicates ubiquinone supplementation
specific_use_cases: Useful when CoQ10 supplementation is being considered
notes: Plasma level may not reflect intracellular status; correlation imperfect.

markers_included: Anti-dopamine D1 anti-D2 anti-lysoganglioside anti-tubulin CaM kinase II stimulation
what_it_measures: Five autoimmune antibodies against brain antigens
what_results_indicate: Positive panel supports PANS/PANDAS diagnosis + IVIG/abx consideration
specific_use_cases: Only commercially-available autoimmune-neuropsychiatric panel; gold standard for PANS/PANDAS workup
contraindications: $495 deposit if commercial insurance; ~50% partial reimbursement; not all insurers pay
notes: Order only if sudden-onset OCD/tics/regression after infection. Don't use as autism screen.

what_results_indicate: >11.5 ng/mL or >20% baseline rise during flare suggests MCAS
specific_use_cases: WHO criterion for systemic mastocytosis; supports MCAS workup
contraindications: Single timepoint can miss episodic MCAS; pair with N-methylhistamine
notes: Baseline + flare-time draw is the WHO MCAS criterion (>20% above baseline +2).

what_results_indicate: Elevated supports MCAS or systemic mastocytosis
specific_use_cases: Pairs with tryptase + prostaglandin D2 for MCAS workup
contraindications: 24h collection burden in young children
notes: May need timed collection during symptomatic flare for highest yield.

what_results_indicate: Elevated supports clonal vs non-clonal MCAS distinction
specific_use_cases: Third MCAS mediator panel
contraindications: Less specific than N-methylhistamine
notes: Positive in all 3 (D2 F2α DM) suggests clonal MCAS.

what_it_measures: Neuroendocrine cell + enterochromaffin marker; secondary mast-cell marker
what_results_indicate: Elevated may indicate MCAS or carcinoid
specific_use_cases: Supplemental MCAS marker
contraindications: PPIs can falsely elevate
notes: Less specific than tryptase but cheaper add-on.

markers_included: qPCR for 16 commensal panels + pathogens + parasites + opportunists + Candida + zonulin + calprotectin + secretory IgA + beta-glucuronidase + steatocrit + occult blood + elastase
what_it_measures: Comprehensive DNA-based stool microbiome + GI function panel
what_results_indicate: Identifies pathogens + dysbiosis + permeability + inflammation
specific_use_cases: Most widely used FM stool panel for autism; qPCR more reliable than culture-based
notes: Add-on zonulin worth ordering. Pair with OAT for full GI picture.

markers_included: Calprotectin EPX secretory IgA pancreatic elastase fecal fats short-chain fatty acids microbiome by PCR + culture parasites zonulin add-on
what_results_indicate: Inflammation + digestion + microbiome + permeability
age_minimum: any but caution under 5 (some markers normally elevated)
specific_use_cases: 3-day collection more sensitive than 1-day; insurance billing option
contraindications: Calprotectin + sIgA elevated baseline in <5yo
notes: $150 copay if billing insurance via Genova. Alternative to GI-MAP.

markers_included: Shotgun metagenomic sequencing of gut microbiome
what_results_indicate: Drives custom probiotic formulation
specific_use_cases: DTC kids/baby option; Floré custom probiotic add-on; ASU autism trial participant
contraindications: Custom probiotic pricing $179/mo subscription is the real cost
notes: Pilot ASU autism trial showed 62% improvement in GI/ASD symptoms with custom probiotic.

what_it_measures: Direct in-vivo test of small intestine barrier integrity
what_results_indicate: Elevated ratio supports leaky gut
specific_use_cases: Direct permeability test; complements zonulin
contraindications: Challenge sugars may cause GI distress in sensitive kids
notes: Zonulin (serum or stool) is faster + simpler if you just want a yes/no.

what_results_indicate: Elevated H2 = bacterial SIBO; CH4 = IMO (intestinal methanogen overgrowth)
specific_use_cases: Distinguishes SIBO vs IMO vs H2S-dominant; guides rifaximin vs neomycin choice
contraindications: Requires 2-3h cooperative breath collection (hard for young/nonverbal)
notes: CPT 91065. Insurance sometimes covers with GI dx.

markers_included: 11 mycotoxins: aflatoxin M1 ochratoxin A sterigmatocystin roridin E verrucarin A enniatin B zearalenone gliotoxin mycophenolic acid dihydrocitrinone chaetoglobosin A
what_results_indicate: Detects mold/mycotoxin body burden
specific_use_cases: Mold exposure relevant to subset; pairs with OAT to detect Aspergillus fumigatus colonization
contraindications: Detection method (ELISA) controversial for some mycotoxins
notes: Order alongside MycoTOX-Aspergillus to distinguish exogenous exposure from colonization.

what_results_indicate: Particularly strong on trichothecene (water-damaged building) detection
specific_use_cases: Strongest panel for water-damaged building exposure
notes: ELISA-based; some labs argue LC-MS/MS (Mosaic Vibrant) is more specific.

markers_included: Mycotoxins + heavy metals + environmental toxins (BPA phthalates glyphosate)
what_results_indicate: Combined mold + heavy metal + chemical body burden
specific_use_cases: LC-MS/MS based; broader exposure assessment
notes: Some panels include genetic variants for detox SNPs.

markers_included: 30+ elements: toxic metals (Pb As Hg Cd Al Sb) + nutrient minerals (Ca Mg Zn Cu Se Cr Mn Mo)
what_it_measures: Hair tissue mineral analysis by ICP-MS
what_results_indicate: Reflects 2-3 month integrated exposure; mineral imbalances
specific_use_cases: Cheap exposure proxy; Cu:Zn ratio informs Walsh PHE-0011 phenotype
contraindications: Reference ranges differ between labs; controversial as diagnostic
notes: Not reliable for diagnosis but useful for tracking response to chelation/zinc.

markers_included: Methylmercury vs inorganic mercury speciation in blood hair urine
what_results_indicate: Distinguishes seafood (methyl) vs amalgam/environmental (inorganic) mercury
specific_use_cases: Only commercially-available mercury speciation test
contraindications: Mercury exposure not autism-causal in mainstream view; subset susceptibility per Hannah Poling
notes: Speciation makes this superior to single-sample mercury tests for source ID.

markers_included: 16 elements: nutrient (Ca Cu Li Mg Mn Mo Se Zn) + toxic (Sb As Cd Co Pb Hg Ag Sr)
what_it_measures: Whole blood toxic + nutrient elements panel
what_results_indicate: Acute/recent exposure to toxic metals
specific_use_cases: Standard exposure screen; complements Tri-Test
contraindications: Reflects recent (weeks) exposure only
notes: Quest/LabCorp also offer Pb As Cd Hg as standard order.

markers_included: Urinary mobilized Pb Hg As Cd Al
what_results_indicate: Contested marker of body burden
age_minimum: clinician-supervised; not for very young or medically fragile
specific_use_cases: Contested in mainstream; reference ranges based on healthy adult provocation aren't standardized
contraindications: DMSA carries risk of mineral depletion + mobilization toxicity; not for impaired excretion
notes: Mainstream medical bodies disavow provoked testing. FM use persists. Consider Quicksilver Tri-Test as less-contested alternative.

markers_included: 35 hormones: cortisol DHEA-S estrogen progesterone testosterone metabolites + melatonin 6-OHMS 8-OHdG + organic acids (MMA xanthurenate HVA VMA pyroglutamate)
what_it_measures: Dried urine sex + adrenal hormones + metabolites + key OAT markers
what_results_indicate: Full HPA-axis + sex hormone + oxidative stress + neurotransmitter
age_minimum: Tanner stage 4+ recommended; sex hormones uninformative pre-puberty
specific_use_cases: Best single test for HPA + sex hormones; not optimal for young children
contraindications: Sex hormone panel uninformative in prepubertal kids
notes: Use DUTCH Adrenal (cortisol-only) variant for pediatric HPA axis screening.

what_it_measures: Diurnal cortisol curve in dried urine
what_results_indicate: HPA-axis dysregulation; flat curve vs inversion vs spike
specific_use_cases: Pediatric-appropriate adrenal-only DUTCH variant
contraindications: Diurnal curve interpretation requires consistent sampling
notes: Cheaper + pediatric-friendly DUTCH option.

what_results_indicate: Same as DUTCH adrenal but saliva
specific_use_cases: Saliva preferred for very young kids who can't void on schedule
notes: Easier collection than DUTCH urine for pre-toilet-trained children.

what_results_indicate: Elevated (>20 µg/dL) supports PHE-0010 pyroluria/kryptopyrroluria
specific_use_cases: DHA Laboratory only US lab providing creatinine-corrected result; Walsh-protocol-aligned
contraindications: Mainstream-disputed entity; sample must be frozen and shielded from light
notes: Sample handling matters more than usual: freeze and ship overnight in opaque container.

what_results_indicate: High = overmethylator; low = undermethylator
specific_use_cases: Walsh-protocol-aligned; used clinically since 1970s for methyl-biotype
contraindications: Whole blood not plasma; specific handling required
notes: Pairs with SAM/SAH ratio for confirmation. Walsh framework dependent.

markers_included: Plasma copper plasma zinc serum ceruloplasmin (calculated free copper)
what_results_indicate: Elevated Cu:Zn ratio (>1.2) supports zinc supplementation
specific_use_cases: Direct test for PHE-0011 Walsh phenotype; insurance-covered
notes: Standard Quest/LabCorp order. Calculate free copper = serum copper - (3 x ceruloplasmin).

what_results_indicate: Low correlates with multiple autism phenotypes; target 40-70 ng/mL
specific_use_cases: Standard order; insurance-covered; LetsGetChecked Everlywell DTC options
contraindications: Mainstream targets (>30 ng/mL) are lower than FM targets (40-70)
notes: Standard of care. Pair with ionized calcium if dosing high.

markers_included: TSH free T4 free T3 reverse T3 anti-TPO anti-Tg
what_results_indicate: Hashimoto's antibodies + functional thyroid status
specific_use_cases: Often missed in pediatric workup; antibodies inform autoimmune trajectory
contraindications: Pediatric thyroid reference ranges differ from adult
notes: Order full panel not just TSH. Anti-TPO+ in mom predicts autoimmune risk in child.

markers_included: Ferritin serum iron TIBC transferrin saturation
what_results_indicate: Low ferritin (<30 ng/mL) drives RLS/sleep/behavior in subset
specific_use_cases: Often overlooked; ferritin <50 worth supplementing in autism + sleep/RLS
contraindications: Acute-phase reactant; check CRP context
notes: Pediatric optimal ferritin ~50-100 ng/mL per most FM guidelines.

what_results_indicate: Rules out anemia infection liver kidney lipid abnormalities
specific_use_cases: Foundation lab; insurance-covered; should be ordered alongside autism workup
notes: Always do this baseline before specialty tests.

what_results_indicate: Elevated inflammation backs PHE-0003 phenotype
specific_use_cases: Cheap inflammation screen
contraindications: Single timepoint can miss episodic inflammation
notes: Add ferritin LDH if mast-cell suspicion.

markers_included: ANA pattern ASCA IgG/IgA anti-TPO anti-Tg
what_results_indicate: Family hx autoimmunity + child elevated antibodies = PHE-0003
specific_use_cases: Standard autoimmune workup; ASCA may indicate Crohn's-pattern gut
notes: Order if family hx autoimmunity or regression-pattern autism.

markers_included: IL-1β IL-6 IL-8 IL-10 IL-17 TNF-α IFN-γ
what_results_indicate: Elevated IL-6 / TNF-α supports neuroinflammation phenotype
specific_use_cases: Mainstream clinical utility limited; FM use widespread
contraindications: Day-to-day variability; expensive; reference ranges contested
notes: Single-timepoint cytokines have known variability problems. Interpret cautiously.

markers_included: IgG IgA against gluten + non-gluten wheat proteins + intestinal barrier markers (zonulin actomyosin LPS)
what_results_indicate: Comprehensive wheat sensitivity + leaky gut markers
specific_use_cases: Bundles wheat reactivity + permeability; widely used to justify GFCF trial
contraindications: IgG sensitivity testing mainstream-disputed
notes: Bundled with Gut Zoomer often at $150 add-on.

markers_included: IgG IgA antibodies against 180 raw/cooked food antigens
what_results_indicate: Identifies dietary triggers (mainstream-disputed for non-celiac)
specific_use_cases: Most-cited IgG food panel; clinical interpretation requires care
contraindications: IgG positivity may reflect tolerance not reactivity; AAAAI does not endorse
notes: Use as input not gospel. Pair with elimination-reintroduction trial.

test_name: True IgE specific food allergy panel
markers_included: IgE antibodies against specific food allergens (milk wheat egg peanut tree nuts soy fish shellfish)
what_results_indicate: Positive supports avoidance + anaphylaxis precaution
specific_use_cases: Mainstream-validated allergy test; distinct from IgG sensitivity
contraindications: Doesn't catch delayed/IgG-mediated reactions
notes: Order alongside IgG panel for full picture. Real anaphylaxis risk vs sensitivity.

markers_included: 24 genes covering psych meds (CYP2D6 CYP2C19 CYP3A4 SLC6A4 HTR2A COMT MTHFR DRD2)
what_it_measures: Pharmacogenomic profile for psych medication selection
what_results_indicate: Guides SSRI/antipsychotic/stimulant choice and dose
specific_use_cases: Useful if child on or starting psychiatric medication; Medicare covers
contraindications: Mainstream skepticism about clinical validity; FDA warning vs unapproved-claim PGx
notes: AACAP guidance: PGx may inform but not dictate prescribing in pediatric psych.

markers_included: Multi-gene panel for psychotropic drug metabolism
what_results_indicate: Same use case as Genomind
specific_use_cases: Most-used PGx panel in US psych; insurance increasingly covers
contraindications: Same FDA caution as Genomind; clinical utility debated
notes: Order through pediatric psych for ADHD/anxiety/depression Rx decisions.

markers_included: 27 genes spanning major drug-metabolizing CYPs + transporters
what_results_indicate: Multi-drug-class metabolizer status
specific_use_cases: Broader drug coverage than Genomind/GeneSight; Mayo collaboration
contraindications: Same FDA caution
notes: Useful if child on complex polypharmacy.

markers_included: FISH probe or MLPA for 22q11.2 region deletion/duplication
what_results_indicate: Diagnoses 22q11.2 deletion syndrome (DiGeorge/VCFS)
specific_use_cases: CMA generally catches this; targeted test useful when CMA negative but high clinical suspicion
contraindications: Insurance approval requires phenotype features (palate cleft cardiac etc)
notes: CMA preferred per 22q.org. Reserve targeted test for confirmation.

what_results_indicate: Diagnoses Rett (~1:10000 females)
specific_use_cases: Indicated for girls with regression hand-stereotypies microcephaly post-1yo
contraindications: WES often catches this; targeted test rare
notes: Often included in autism gene panels.

what_results_indicate: Diagnoses Angelman
specific_use_cases: Methylation-based test catches most cases; sequencing catches rest
notes: Indicated for happy-disposition + severe DD + seizures.

what_results_indicate: Diagnoses TSC (mTOR pathway syndromic phenotype)
specific_use_cases: Indicated for hypopigmented macules cardiac rhabdomyomas seizures cortical tubers
notes: Rapamycin FDA-approved for TSC; positive result has direct treatment implication.

what_results_indicate: Diagnoses PTEN-related autism (often with macrocephaly)
specific_use_cases: Indicated for macrocephaly + ASD; mTOR pathway implications
notes: Often included in autism gene panels.

markers_included: Phenylketonuria MSUD biotinidase hypothyroidism CAH hemoglobinopathies many IEMs
what_results_indicate: Catch missed inborn errors of metabolism
specific_use_cases: Records request to state lab; often already done but family doesn't have copy
contraindications: Most states require parental consent for follow-up testing
notes: Free or low-cost. Always start here to confirm no missed IEM.

what_results_indicate: Identifies deficits driving anxiety/sleep/behavior
specific_use_cases: Standard order; cheap; useful in all FM workups
contraindications: Serum minerals don't always reflect tissue status
notes: RBC Mg + serum zinc are the most informative routine orders.

what_results_indicate: Low correlates with anxiety sleep dysregulation muscle tension
specific_use_cases: More sensitive than serum Mg
notes: Optimal ~6.0-6.5 mg/dL per most FM ranges.

what_results_indicate: Low B6 = pyroluria + transsulfuration insufficiency
specific_use_cases: Active B6; more informative than serum total B6
notes: Pair with kryptopyrrole if pyroluria suspected.

markers_included: Omega-3 (EPA DHA) omega-6 (AA LA) plus AA:EPA ratio omega-3 index
what_results_indicate: Low omega-3 + high omega-6 supports DHA supplementation
specific_use_cases: OmegaQuant offers finger-stick at-home DTC option
notes: Useful for tracking DHA supplementation response.

markers_included: Reduced GSH oxidized GSSG GSH:GSSG ratio
what_results_indicate: Low GSH or low ratio supports NAC + glycine + glutamine
specific_use_cases: James 2004+ autism biomarker framework central
contraindications: Sample handling critical (rapid freezing)
notes: Pairs with cysteine sulfate methionine in James biochemistry panel.

what_results_indicate: Elevated = active oxidative stress
specific_use_cases: Included in DUTCH Complete; standalone available
notes: Reasonable serial monitor of oxidative-stress response to intervention.

what_results_indicate: Gold-standard oxidative stress marker
specific_use_cases: Most rigorous oxidative-stress marker; less commonly ordered
notes: Mayo's preferred oxidative stress marker. Insurance sometimes covers.

what_results_indicate: Low total IgA/IgG = humoral immune deficit; total IgE elevation = atopy
specific_use_cases: Identifies CVID IgA deficiency atopic baseline
notes: Standard immunology screen.

what_results_indicate: Identifies IgG subclass-specific immune deficit
specific_use_cases: Required before IVIG consideration
notes: Order before any IVIG decision.

what_results_indicate: Elevated supports PANDAS workup
specific_use_cases: Cheap PANS/PANDAS screen; do before Cunningham Panel
notes: Cunningham + ASO + anti-DNase B together is the standard PANDAS panel.

what_results_indicate: Elevated supports PANS workup
specific_use_cases: Common PANS trigger; included in standard PANS workup
notes: Order alongside Cunningham + ASO if PANS suspected.

markers_included: Anti-Borrelia IgG IgM Western blot bands + co-infection panel
what_results_indicate: Positive supports Lyme-driven PANS phenotype
specific_use_cases: Contested in mainstream; IGeneX criteria broader than CDC
contraindications: Mainstream uses CDC two-tier interpretation; IGeneX criteria expansive
notes: Only order if tick-exposure history + sudden-onset symptoms.

what_results_indicate: Low = immune-compromised gut; high = active immune response
specific_use_cases: Often included in GI-MAP or Genova GI panels
contraindications: Pediatric reference ranges differ
notes: Single-test rarely worth ordering standalone; bundle with stool panel.

what_results_indicate: Elevated = IBD/EoE/IBD-like; rule out organic GI disease
specific_use_cases: Gold-standard inflammatory bowel screen; insurance-covered
contraindications: Pediatric reference ~normally elevated under 5yo
notes: Order before invasive endoscopy. Elevated >250 ug/g indicates GI workup.

what_results_indicate: Elevated supports leaky gut
specific_use_cases: Increasingly contested (some labs measure protein cross-reactant not zonulin)
notes: Newer assays (LPS Cyrex Array 2) may be more specific.

what_results_indicate: More specific than zonulin alone for leaky gut
specific_use_cases: Multi-marker approach to leaky gut
notes: More information dense than single-marker zonulin but pricier.

what_results_indicate: Low elastase = pancreatic insufficiency; positive blood = GI lesion
specific_use_cases: Often included in comprehensive stool panels
notes: Low elastase explains chronic FTT/malabsorption picture.

what_results_indicate: Rules out epileptiform activity; abnormal in 50-80% of autism
specific_use_cases: Indicated when seizures suspected; ESES/CSWS rule-out
contraindications: Insurance approval requires clinical justification (seizure or regression)
notes: Always order if regression + nocturnal events or staring spells.

what_it_measures: Brain rhythms compared to normative database
what_results_indicate: Identifies hypoconnectivity / atypical rhythms; guides neurofeedback
specific_use_cases: FM-favored; not insurance-covered; clinical utility debated
contraindications: Mainstream considers experimental for clinical decision-making
notes: Often paired with neurofeedback. Document baseline before neurofeedback trial.

markers_included: EEG EMG EOG ECG O2 sat respirations video
what_results_indicate: Identifies OSA periodic limb movement REM behavior disorder
specific_use_cases: Often missed; OSA in autism 30-65%; CPAP/T&A can dramatically improve behavior
contraindications: Sleep study burden in young children
notes: Especially indicated if loud snoring + behavior dysregulation.

what_it_measures: Detects cortical malformations tumors tubers (TSC) lesions
what_results_indicate: Rules out structural cause of regression/seizures
specific_use_cases: Only indicated if neuro-focal signs micro/macrocephaly seizures regression
contraindications: Sedation risk in young children
notes: Not routine for ASD without neurologic red flags.

what_results_indicate: Atypical MoM values predict autism risk (BIO-0179)
specific_use_cases: Free if mom requests records from her OB
contraindications: May not be retrievable for kids >5-10yo
notes: Direct relevance per atlas BIO-0179 + HYP-0073.

what_results_indicate: Low PAPP-A predicts autism risk subset (BIO-0180)
specific_use_cases: Free if retrievable from OB records
contraindications: Records may not be available for older children
notes: Direct relevance per atlas BIO-0180.

markers_included: Cord arterial pH base excess APGARs 1+5min
what_results_indicate: Acidemia or low APGAR supports HIE susceptibility (HYP-0040)
specific_use_cases: Free; identifies subclinical HIE / perinatal hypoxia
contraindications: May not be in older records
notes: Cord pH <7.10 or 5-min APGAR <7 = HYP-0040 susceptibility tier.

markers_included: HRV resting HR HRV body temperature sleep stages
what_results_indicate: Tracks autonomic balance + sleep
specific_use_cases: Consumer-grade not clinical
contraindications: Not validated in pediatric ASD
notes: Useful for older / cooperative kids to track autonomic patterns + sleep.

markers_included: HRV ECG O2 sat sleep stages
what_results_indicate: Tracks HRV sleep autonomic patterns
specific_use_cases: Consumer-grade
contraindications: Not validated for clinical ASD use
notes: Useful for cooperative older child / parent tracking.

what_results_indicate: Tracks autonomic balance
specific_use_cases: Subscription model $30/mo
contraindications: Adult-targeted; pediatric use off-label
notes: For older cooperative teens with anxiety/regulation issues.

markers_included: Epigenetic biological age + DunedinPACE pace of aging
what_results_indicate: Tracks rate of aging response to lifestyle/interventions
specific_use_cases: Validated 20-70yo only; pediatric use experimental
contraindications: Not validated for pediatric autism
notes: Use for parental health tracking; rarely informative for child themselves.

what_it_measures: Epigenetic + microbiome saliva signature for ASD diagnosis
what_results_indicate: 82% sensitivity 88% specificity for ASD in 18mo-6yo
specific_use_cases: Aid-in-diagnosis; FDA Breakthrough Device
contraindications: Not a standalone diagnostic; mainstream skepticism
notes: Recent (2020 launch); not yet standard of care.

what_results_indicate: Metabotyping into autism phenotype clusters
specific_use_cases: Stratifies into 4 metabotypes
contraindications: Not yet widely validated; access limited
notes: Metabotype output potentially actionable; expensive.

test_name: DDI Doctor's Data Comprehensive Stool Analysis (CSA)
markers_included: Beneficial flora opportunistic flora parasites pH SCFA chymotrypsin elastase fecal fats meat fibers calprotectin lactoferrin
what_results_indicate: Alternative comprehensive GI panel
specific_use_cases: Older approach (culture-based); GI-MAP uses qPCR
contraindications: Culture misses non-culturable organisms
notes: GI-MAP often preferred for autism workup now.

what_results_indicate: Urinary AA panel complement
specific_use_cases: Less commonly ordered now; supplanted by OAT
notes: Plasma AA is preferred; urinary AA niche.

what_results_indicate: Identifies inflammatory food reactions
specific_use_cases: Older approach; mainstream-disputed
contraindications: Method (leukocyte response) less validated than IgG ELISA
notes: Cyrex / Vibrant generally preferred over ALCAT now.

what_results_indicate: Mainstream-disputed clinical utility
specific_use_cases: IgG4 may represent tolerance rather than reaction
contraindications: AAAAI does not endorse for food sensitivity
notes: Standard Quest order but interpretation controversial.

test_name: Mosaic Diagnostics IgG food allergy panel
what_results_indicate: Same use case as Cyrex Array 10
specific_use_cases: DTC option for IgG food testing
contraindications: Same mainstream concerns about IgG specificity
notes: Cheaper than Cyrex Array 10 with similar interpretation framework.

markers_included: Intracellular vitamins minerals antioxidants amino acids (~35 markers)
what_results_indicate: Direct measure of cellular nutrient deficits
specific_use_cases: Lymphocyte-based; reflects 4-6mo nutrient status
contraindications: Some studies dispute the cell-growth proxy methodology
notes: Useful when serum panels look fine but symptoms persist.

markers_included: ~60 metabolic conditions: PKU MSUD organic acidurias FAO defects galactosemia biotinidase CAH hemoglobinopathies hypothyroidism CF SCID
what_it_measures: Newborn-period IEM + endocrinopathy + immune deficiency screen
what_results_indicate: Catches treatable inborn errors before symptomatic onset
specific_use_cases: Standard of care; results in newborn record
contraindications: Mostly already done; retrieve records
notes: Always retrieve. Some states more comprehensive than others.

markers_included: C22 C24 C26 fatty acids C24/C22 + C26/C22 ratios
what_results_indicate: Elevated VLCFA = ALD Refsum or peroxisomal disorder
specific_use_cases: Indicated for regression + adrenal insufficiency or seizures + leukodystrophy on MRI
notes: Rare cause but treatable; X-linked ALD especially.

what_results_indicate: Elevated ammonia = urea cycle or mito overflow; low uric acid = molybdenum/sulfite oxidase issue
specific_use_cases: Often included in CMP; quick rule-out for urea cycle issues
notes: Ammonia must be drawn properly (no tourniquet/iced).

markers_included: HLA-B27 for spondyloarthritis; HLA-DR/DQ haplotype for celiac + Shoemaker chronic inflammatory response
what_results_indicate: DR DQ subtypes can indicate celiac risk; CIRS-mold susceptibility
specific_use_cases: Shoemaker mold-susceptibility HLA framework contested
contraindications: Genotype is fixed; only useful once
notes: Order if mold-exposure phenotype + recurrent CIRS suspected.

what_results_indicate: Positive indicates eradication therapy
specific_use_cases: Often comorbid with chronic GI symptoms
notes: Insurance-covered; standard pediatric GI workup item.

what_results_indicate: Long-COVID neuropsychiatric subset
specific_use_cases: Newer; relevance to post-COVID neurodev regression cases
contraindications: Methodology + clinical interpretation still maturing
notes: Only relevant for documented post-COVID regression.

test_name: Total mast cell tryptase + bone marrow KIT mutation (when indicated)
what_results_indicate: Diagnoses systemic mastocytosis
specific_use_cases: Reserve for high tryptase + skin lesions
contraindications: Marrow biopsy is invasive
notes: Not routine; reserve for confirmed mastocytosis suspicion.

what_results_indicate: Identifies single-gene autism causes more cheaply than WES
specific_use_cases: Cheaper than WES but lower yield; some labs offer at $0 patient cost
notes: Invitae Behind the Seizure offers free testing in specific clinical contexts. Multi-vendor — choose by insurance coverage; lab consolidation in market.

what_results_indicate: Glycan age as inflammation/aging proxy
specific_use_cases: Not pediatric-validated
contraindications: Adult only
notes: For parental health tracking not child.

what_results_indicate: Elevated indicates impaired estrogen / toxin conjugation
specific_use_cases: Bundle marker in comprehensive stool panels
notes: Typically bundled in GI-MAP / GI Effects pricing.

markers_included: Lactate pyruvate L:P + carnitine + acylcarnitines bundled
what_results_indicate: One-order mito screen
specific_use_cases: Bundled = cheaper than ordering separately
notes: Verify pre-analytical handling for lactate (deproteinize promptly).

what_results_indicate: Low arginine vasopressin predicts intranasal AVP response (Parker 2019)
specific_use_cases: Research-grade; access limited
contraindications: Sample-handling sensitive
notes: Stanford-led research grade; not standard clinical.

test_name: Direct PCR strep throat (if PANS suspected)
what_results_indicate: Positive supports PANDAS workup
specific_use_cases: Standard pediatric office test; rule out active strep before chronic-PANDAS workup
notes: Sometimes underused. Document strep history.

what_results_indicate: Alternative to GI-MAP with broader microbe coverage
specific_use_cases: Broader pathogen coverage than GI-MAP; LC-MS/MS based
contraindications: Less practitioner experience than GI-MAP
notes: Newer entrant; gaining FM adoption.

what_results_indicate: Cheap DTC microbiome composition
specific_use_cases: Cheap consumer option
contraindications: Less clinical depth than GI-MAP/Genova
notes: Useful for cost-conscious initial screen; upgrade if findings warrant.

what_results_indicate: More markers + arguably more specific than ELISA
specific_use_cases: LC-MS/MS vs ELISA debate active
notes: Some labs prefer LC-MS/MS for specificity vs Mosaic ELISA.

what_results_indicate: Identifies PHE-0007 phenotype candidates
specific_use_cases: Research-grade interpretation overlay
contraindications: Specialty-clinic access limited
notes: Lemonnier-style framework; not yet standard.

what_it_measures: Opioid-like peptides from incomplete dairy/gluten digestion
what_results_indicate: Elevated supports GFCF diet trial
specific_use_cases: Theory popular in FM autism community; mainstream skeptical
contraindications: Methodology criticized; reproducibility issues
notes: Frequently cited but evidence quality contested. GFCF trial is the practical test.

markers_included: Complex I + II/III + IV respiratory chain enzyme activity + citrate synthase (mito content)
what_it_measures: Buccal mucosa mitochondrial respiratory chain enzyme activity normalized to mitochondrial content
what_results_indicate: Detects functional mitochondrial dysfunction; 84% correlation with muscle-biopsy gold standard
specific_use_cases: Suspected mito dysfunction with poor blood-test resolution; non-invasive alternative to muscle biopsy; pre-/post-mito cocktail trial
contraindications: None
notes: Major catalog gap fill. Religen 2023 validation study showed 84% concordance with muscle biopsy. Highest-yield non-invasive mito test.

provider: Mayo Clinic Labs / Baylor / specialty children's hospital
what_it_measures: Definitive marker for cerebral folate deficiency
what_results_indicate: Low CSF 5-MTHF with normal serum folate = cerebral folate deficiency (treatable with leucovorin)
specific_use_cases: FRAA-positive child with partial leucovorin response; severe regression; seizures of unclear origin
contraindications: Lumbar puncture aversion limits use; sedation often required
notes: The only definitive test for cerebral folate deficiency. Directly anchors the INT-0001 (CSRS 83.35) calibration logic. Under-utilized despite high clinical value.

what_it_measures: CSF dopamine + serotonin metabolites + BH4 cofactor pathway
what_results_indicate: Catches AADC deficiency / sepiapterin reductase / DHPR deficiency — all treatable monogenic ID/ASD mimics
specific_use_cases: Suspected dopamine-serotonin biosynthesis defect; severe early regression with movement features; failed standard psychiatric workup
contraindications: LP aversion; sedation
notes: Catches the highest-impact treatable ASD-mimic conditions. Often bundled with CSF 5-MTHF in same lumbar puncture.

markers_included: Pediatric eye-tracking gaze patterns vs normative ASD model
what_it_measures: Visual social-attention gaze patterns compared against ASD-normative population data
what_results_indicate: Aid-in-diagnosis ASD signal; reduces diagnostic-odyssey time-to-evaluation
specific_use_cases: Age 16 months to 8 years where DSM workup is constrained or wait-listed
contraindications: Not standalone diagnostic; requires clinical correlation
notes: Only FDA-cleared eye-tracking diagnostic for ASD. 2023 initial clearance expanded to age 8 in 2026.

markers_included: Laser-ablation ICP-MS strand-by-strand exposure pattern + ASD ML score
what_it_measures: Time-resolved exposure-pattern signature across hair growth period (~3 months) with ML-derived ASD-risk score
what_results_indicate: Aid-in-ruling-out ASD; 90% sensitivity 95% NPV per FDA filing
specific_use_cases: Age 1 month to 10 years; available by prescription in 49 states as of 2026; aid-in-ruling-out tool
contraindications: Mainstream skepticism remains despite FDA receptiveness; not standalone diagnostic
notes: Novel methodology applying laser-ablation ICP-MS to single hair strand. Same approach being explored for ALS prodromal detection.

markers_included: Shotgun metagenomic strain-level + age-specific reference ranges
what_it_measures: Age-stratified pediatric microbiome (0-4mo / 4-12mo / 1-3yr / 3-18yr) at strain resolution
what_results_indicate: Identifies dysbiosis with age-appropriate reference; published infant eczema RCT showed 83% reduction signal
specific_use_cases: Infant or toddler microbiome baseline; post-antibiotic recovery; eczema or allergic-march risk; younger than most other panels accommodate
contraindications: None
notes: Best pediatric-specific microbiome test on the market. Fills the 0-4 year gap that adult-targeted Viome and Biomesight cannot serve.

markers_included: 125+ markers: organic acids + plasma AAs + EFAs + oxidative stress + micronutrients + heavy metals
what_results_indicate: One-order bundle covering OAT + plasma AAs + EFAs + oxidative stress + minerals — replaces 5+ separate test orders
specific_use_cases: Comprehensive baseline FM panel as alternative to ordering 5+ separate tests
contraindications: Urine collection burden in young child
notes: Consolidated test that lowers parent friction. Often cheaper through Genova Connect insurance pathway.

what_it_measures: Confirmation of Phelan-McDermid spectrum / 22q13 deletion syndrome
what_results_indicate: Confirms Phelan-McDermid; informs IGF-1 trial eligibility and natural-history surveillance
specific_use_cases: ASD + severe expressive language delay + hypotonia + characteristic facies; CMA negative for 22q13.3 deletion
contraindications: None
notes: SHANK3 haploinsufficiency triggers IGF-1 clinical trials and Phelan-McDermid Foundation natural-history registry enrollment.

markers_included: ADNP de novo variants (most common dominant-monogenic autism syndrome)
what_results_indicate: Confirms Helsmoortel-Van der Aa syndrome; eligibility for emerging davunetide / NAP peptide trials
specific_use_cases: ASD + ID + characteristic facies + early tooth eruption + chronic infections + GI dysmotility
contraindications: None
notes: Most common dominant-monogenic autism (~0.17%). Emerging davunetide trial enrollment criterion.

what_results_indicate: Confirms CHD8-related ASD subgroup (~0.2% of ASD); informs surveillance
specific_use_cases: Macrocephaly + ASD + GI dysmotility + sleep disorder + tall stature
contraindications: None
notes: Largest-effect autism gene typically reflexed via WES; standalone available for confirmation testing.

what_it_measures: Insulin-like growth factor 1 and its primary binding protein
what_results_indicate: Identifies functional GH-axis dysregulation; lower in preschool ASD per Shen 2024; relevant to SHANK3/Phelan-McDermid IGF-1 trials
specific_use_cases: Growth faltering OR macrosomia + macrocephaly; Phelan-McDermid pre-trial baseline
contraindications: None
notes: Catches functional GH-axis dysregulation. Shen 2024 multi-site preschool ASD data showed lower IGF-1.

what_results_indicate: Identifies vitamin A deficiency; Cheng 2021 multi-center data show correlation with ASD severity in boys
specific_use_cases: Restricted-eater autism child; pre-/post-cod-liver-oil trial; pica
contraindications: None
notes: Foundational fat-soluble vitamin baseline missing from prior catalog. Cheap; insurance-billable; Cheng 2021 n=3300 China data show correlation with severity in boys.

what_results_indicate: Identifies vitamin E deficiency (common in restricted eaters / fat-malabsorption)
specific_use_cases: Restricted-eater autism child; fat-malabsorption suspicion
contraindications: None
notes: Foundational fat-soluble vitamin baseline. Common comorbidity in pediatric ASD restricted-eater phenotype.

what_it_measures: Whole mitochondrial genome sequencing with heteroplasmy detection
what_results_indicate: Catches MELAS / MERRF / LHON / Leigh syndrome variants invisible to WES
specific_use_cases: Regression + lactate elevation + L:P ratio >25 + matrilineal-pattern family history
contraindications: None
notes: Hannah Poling-class evaluation. Most pediatric mito disease is nuclear-encoded but mtDNA panel still essential for the matrilineal-regression subset.

markers_included: OAT + mycotoxins + glyphosate + 173 environmental pollutants (BPA + phthalates + parabens + organophosphates + PFAS)
what_it_measures: Consolidated organic acids + mold + agricultural + industrial chemical exposure panel
what_results_indicate: Comprehensive exposure quantification (not diagnosis) for high-exposure or refractory-phenotype workup
specific_use_cases: Comprehensive toxicant exposure baseline; high environmental exposure suspicion; refractory phenotype after standard workup
contraindications: Cost; interpretation requires FM expertise
notes: One-order replacement for ordering OAT + MycoTOX + Glyphosate + GPL-TOX separately. Contested causal interpretation; quantification methodology mainstream-validated.

test_name: Anti-neural autoimmune encephalitis panel (NMDAR + GAD65 + VGKC)
markers_included: Anti-NMDAR + anti-GAD65 + voltage-gated K-channel + anti-AMPA + anti-GABA-B receptor antibodies
what_results_indicate: Catches treatable autoimmune encephalitis ASD-mimic (anti-NMDAR especially) — IVIG / rituximab responsive
specific_use_cases: Acute psychiatric/cognitive regression in previously typical child; status of unclear etiology
contraindications: Lumbar puncture for CSF component
notes: Critical to rule in/out anti-NMDAR encephalitis. Treatable with IVIG/steroids/rituximab. Highest-yield single test in acute regression workup.

markers_included: EDA (electrodermal activity) + heart rate + accelerometry + temperature; FDA-cleared seizure detection algorithm
what_it_measures: Continuous physiological monitoring with seizure detection alert
what_results_indicate: FDA-cleared for generalized tonic-clonic seizure detection
specific_use_cases: Suspected nocturnal or silent seizures in non-verbal child; documented epilepsy comorbidity
contraindications: Subscription cost; child may not tolerate wear
notes: Complements polysomnography for the silent-seizure subset of regressive ASD. Real-time alerts to parents.

provider: Doctor's Data / LabCorp / Laboratoire Philippe Auguste
what_results_indicate: Identifies chronic mercury / arsenic exposure pattern without provocation challenge
specific_use_cases: Confirmed/suspected mercury or arsenic exposure as non-provoked alternative to DMSA challenge
contraindications: None
notes: Non-provoked
key_pmids: mainstream-published heavy-metal biomarker. Safer than DMSA challenge already in catalog.,20077233

markers_included: ~20000 protein-coding genes; clinical-grade variant interpretation; SFARI Tier 1+2 gene curation; entry to Simons Searchlight
what_it_measures: Whole exome sequencing returned to family with board-certified genetic counselor interpretation
what_results_indicate: Identifies clinically actionable rare and de novo variants; ~10-15% diagnostic yield; opens door to gene-specific natural history studies and trial matching
specific_use_cases: Every autism family eligible should enroll; free comprehensive WES; reanalyzed against new SFARI gene findings annually
contraindications: None — non-invasive sample collection
notes: Lead test — recommend before any paid genetic testing. Slow turnaround (6-12 months) so order alongside fast options.

provider: Mom's Lab (Van de Water UC Davis) / Pediatric Bioscience
markers_included: Autoantibodies to fetal brain proteins: LDH-A LDH-B STIP1 CRMP1 CRMP2 GDA YBOX
what_it_measures: Maternal autoantibodies that cross placenta and bind fetal brain targets during gestation
what_results_indicate: Indicates MAR-autism subtype (~10-20% of cases); informs Hannah-Poling-style P-component susceptibility framing for current and future pregnancies
specific_use_cases: Closes largest P-component gap in Hannah Poling framework; complements FRAA on susceptibility axis
contraindications: Pregnancy and current/planned pregnancy contexts
notes: Van de Water lab is primary academic source; commercial assays variable in specificity. Test the mother not the child.
